commands.review_summary:
The PR branch adds Linux fnm alias PATH entries and also changes ACP session limits, Feishu/Lark cron target normalization, Slack channel type normalization, and Telegram chat-not-found input previews.

Reproducibility: yes. for review purposes: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/68169 has concrete fnm/doctor steps, and the PR's daemon omission plus Telegram redaction-order issue are source-reproducible from PR head and current-main comparison. I did not run live behavior tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the branch is conflicting, lacks contributor proof, and mixes obsolete daemon work with security-sensitive ACP/Telegram changes.
